front: What is the Cartesian Product of two sets A and B?
back: The Cartesian Product A × B is the set of all ordered pairs (a, b) where a is an element of A and b is an element of B. Formally, A × B = {(a, b) | a ∈ A and b ∈ B}.

front: Define a binary relation R from set A to set B.
back: A binary relation R from set A to set B is a subset of the Cartesian Product A × B. If (a, b) is an element of R, we say 'a is related to b' and write a R b.

front: What are the key properties a relation R on a set A can have? (Name at least 3)
back: Key properties include: Reflexive (∀a∈A, aRa), Symmetric (if aRb then bRa), Antisymmetric (if aRb and bRa then a=b), and Transitive (if aRb and bRc then aRc).

front: What is the difference between a relation and a function?
back: A function is a special type of relation from set A to set B where every element in A is related to exactly one element in B. A general relation can have an element in A related to zero, one, or many elements in B.

front: How do you find the inverse of a relation R?
back: The inverse of a relation R, denoted R⁻¹, is created by reversing each ordered pair in R. If R ⊆ A × B, then R⁻¹ ⊆ B × A and R⁻¹ = {(b, a) | (a, b) ∈ R}.
